title: run gitlab-ce as a container gives error message from podman about .service not found
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

starting gitlab-ce container results int:
```
$ docker start gitlab
ERRO[0000] exit status 1                                
ERRO[0000] Unit 57d34b8bc094a8ff6a7f274218e7a46d2b5975c2a5c8ee1797911cb78eac560f.service not found. 
gitlab
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.
```
GITLAB_HOME=/home/podman; docker run --hostname host.domain.de --env GITLAB_OMNIBUS_CONFIG="external_url 'http://host.domain.de:2080/';" --publish 443:443 --publish 2080:2080 --publish 2022:22   --name gitlab   --restart always   --volume $GITLAB_HOME/gitlab/config:/etc/gitlab   --volume $GITLAB_HOME/gitlab/logs:/var/log/gitlab   --volume $GITLAB_HOME/gitlab/data:/var/opt/gitlab   gitlab/gitlab-ce:latest                     
ERRO[0000] exit status 1                                
ERRO[0000] Unit 57d34b8bc094a8ff6a7f274218e7a46d2b5975c2a5c8ee1797911cb78eac560f.service not found. 

```

**Describe the results you received:**

see above

**Describe the results you expected:**

no error message

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
$ podman version
Version:            1.8.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.11.6
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```
... --publish 443:443 --publish 80:80 --publish 8022:22   ...

```

only 80 is / was taken
That would certainly be a bug.
You mean both or the port problem? I see the port problem only on the gitlab container and this is the only container where i see the service problem as well. Other containers work fine...
Yes multipl --port options not working. If this works on other containers then it is specific to the image.
Works on every image without this .service error - so i'll wait until this is fixed.
Any chance to workaround this one? Currently I can‘t use that container.
@disaster123 Can you provide a `podman inspect` of the failing container?

Aha, it's not auto-update - it's a healthcheck.

@baude Are healthcheck failures intended to be fatal?
Yes I can in a few hours.otherwise it’s the official container of gitlab So you can just do it on your machine if is it is easier
